title: lavandula dentata var. vulgaris
intro: Lavandula dentata var. vulgaris, also known as French lavender, is a subspecies of the lavender plant with linear, lance-shaped leaves and a distinctive fragrance. This plant is renowned for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and calming effects due to the presence of essential oils like linalool and linalyl acetate. The health benefits of French lavender include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Lavandula dentata var. vulgaris can be used in various herbal preparations, such as essential oils, teas, and potpourri, to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
